message:
feat(efun): port NetBSD efun(3) family of exit-on-failure wrappers (bd-6i7pl)

Adds the NetBSD libutil "exit-on-failure" wrapper family. Each
wrapper calls the corresponding plain libc function and, on
failure, invokes a globally-installed error callback. The default
callback writes a brief diagnostic to stderr and exits with
EXIT_FAILURE; callers can install a custom callback via
`esetfunc` that returns normally — in which case the wrapper also
returns the underlying NULL/0 failure indicator, matching BSD
behavior.

New C entry points:

- esetfunc(efunc) — install/clear the global error callback;
  returns the previous callback (or NULL).
- estrlcpy(dst, src, dstsize)
- estrlcat(dst, src, dstsize)
- estrdup(s)
- estrndup(s, maxlen)
- ecalloc(nmemb, size)
- emalloc(size)
- erealloc(ptr, size)
- efopen(path, mode)

Implementation:

- New `efun_abi` module gated behind `cfg(not(test))` (matches the
  surrounding modules whose no_mangle symbols would conflict in
  unit-test builds).
- EFunc type matches NetBSD's variadic
  `void (*)(int, const char *, ...)`. Wrappers always invoke the
  callback with no varargs (a pre-formatted message as the format
  string), so existing C callbacks that simply call vfprintf see
  the diagnostic verbatim.
- malloc(0) / calloc(0,*) / realloc(_,0) / *(*,0) cases are not
  treated as failures (libc allowing NULL for zero-size requests).

Tests: 19 integration tests using a transmuted non-variadic test
callback covering: esetfunc save/restore/clear, success
passthrough for every wrapper, callback invocation on NULL inputs,
allocation failure simulation via overflow guards, ecalloc
zero-fill, erealloc preserves data + grows, estrlcpy/estrlcat
overflow detection, efopen success/nonexistent/NULL paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-6azoc","title":"syscall: timer_settime deleted timer handle with null new_value should still return EINVAL exactly","description":"Host glibc returns EINVAL for timer_settime(timer_id, 0, NULL, NULL) after timer_delete(timer_id) succeeds, so the deleted-handle contract dominates the null-new-value path. Add ABI regression coverage for that precedence.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:40:03.555628876Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T04:45:59.995053356Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T04:45:59.992032758Z","close_reason":"timer_settime(deleted, NULL, NULL) → EINVAL pinned (deleted-handle dominates null new_value).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-6i7pl","title":"PORT: efun(3) family (esetfunc + ealloc/edup/efopen wrappers)","description":"Port the NetBSD efun(3) family of "exit-on-failure" wrappers: esetfunc, estrlcpy, estrlcat, estrdup, estrndup, ecalloc, emalloc, erealloc, efopen. Each wraps the corresponding plain libc function and, on failure, invokes a globally-installed error callback (default: write a brief diagnostic to stderr + exit(EXIT_FAILURE)). esetfunc(efunc) swaps the global callback and returns the previous one. The callback type matches NetBSDs `void (*)(int, const char *, ...)` but is invoked with a pre-formatted message (no varargs from Rust). Tests: success passthrough mirrors plain libc, failure invokes installed callback rather than exiting, esetfunc returns prior callback, NULL src/dst guards.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T08:14:56.031142956Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T08:14:56.031142956Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-6j9w","title":"syscall: process_vm_readv/writev invalid flags should override all-NULL zero-count fast path","description":"Host glibc returns EINVAL, not success, when process_vm_readv/process_vm_writev receive flags!=0 even if both iov pointers are NULL and both counts are zero. Add ABI regression coverage for that precedence rule so flag validation cannot be bypassed by the degenerate no-op path.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:20:28.678323108Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T19:04:20.458302241Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T19:04:20.456206294Z","close_reason":"Added host-parity ABI coverage proving invalid process_vm flags override the all-NULL zero-count no-op path with exact EINVAL","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Add an exact ABI regression assertion so message-send null-payload handling stops hiding behind a broad errno family.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:26:15.934020928Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T20:16:36.884354820Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:16:36.882046430Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-6kwnr","title":"[HIGH] pthread_cond_init rejects a host-initialized pthread_condattr_t with EINVAL (2 tests deterministically fail)","description":"## Symptom

`cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_cond_core_test` has two deterministic failures:

```
test condvar_init_accepts_initialized_attr ... FAILED
test condvar_init_accepts_monotonic_attr_clock ... FAILED

thread 'condvar_init_accepts_initialized_attr' panicked at crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/pthread_cond_core_test.rs:86:9:
assertion `left == right` failed
  left: 22
 right: 0

thread 'condvar_init_accepts_monotonic_attr_clock' panicked at crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/pthread_cond_core_test.rs:107:9:
assertion `left == right` failed
  left: 22
 right: 0

test result: FAILED. 16 passed; 2 failed
```

rc=22 is EINVAL. Our `pthread_cond_init` is rejecting the `pthread_condattr_t` pointer with EINVAL where the test expects success (rc=0).

## Test bodies (authoritative — not me)

crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/pthread_cond_core_test.rs:80 (condvar_init_accepts_initialized_attr):
```rust
let mut attr: libc::pthread_condattr_t = unsafe { std::mem::zeroed() };
assert_eq!(libc::pthread_condattr_init(&mut attr), 0);          // HOST libc init
assert_eq!(pthread_cond_init(cond, &attr), 0);                  // OURS — FAILS here, got 22
assert_eq!(pthread_cond_destroy(cond), 0);
assert_eq!(libc::pthread_condattr_destroy(&mut attr), 0);
```

crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/pthread_cond_core_test.rs:96 (condvar_init_accepts_monotonic_attr_clock): same pattern, plus libc::pthread_condattr_setclock(&mut attr, CLOCK_MONOTONIC).

## Diagnosis

The tests use HOST libc's `pthread_condattr_init` / `pthread_condattr_setclock` to populate `attr`, then pass that `attr` pointer to OUR `pthread_cond_init`. Our impl validates the incoming attr against its own expected layout / sentinel bytes and returns EINVAL because the host-initialized bytes don't match.

This is a cross-impl boundary question rather than a pure regression — the test fixture deliberately mixes host-initialized attrs with our cond_init because that is the real-world LD_PRELOAD pattern: user code links against host libc's condattr_init at compile time but our pthread_cond_init interposes at link time. If our cond_init rejects host-initialized condattrs in production, every real user program breaks under FRANKENLIBC_MODE=hardened.

## Fix direction

Two options:

1. **Relax validation**: treat any non-null `attr` pointer as acceptable, pull the clock / pshared via portable accessors (`pthread_condattr_getclock` / `getpshared`) instead of inspecting our own layout. This matches glibc's own behavior — glibc never rejects a validly-constructed condattr of either origin.
2. **Pass-through**: when the attr's layout doesn't match our internal format, fall through to host `pthread_cond_init` the same way bd-21ypi (138c07c3) routes non-native pthread_t handles. Consistent with the existing host-handoff pattern.

Option 1 is simpler; option 2 matches the existing architectural pattern for 'didn't originate in us'.

## Provenance

Found by cc-libc FULL REVIEW MODE at HEAD 6c0380b4 br-bd-qhnxc: dispatch host pthread cancellation. The bd-qhnxc fix unblocked pthread_abi_test, which let cargo test --workspace --all-targets get further than before and expose this second pthread-cluster failure. Fix commits previously landing on the condvar path: 2e1d65bc 'test(abi): expand pthread condvar core tests', d7b4a832 'expand pthread condvar and thread lifecycle test coverage' — so the tests are intentional coverage, not accidental.

## Blast radius

Two deterministic failures in crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/pthread_cond_core_test.rs. Blocks frankenlibc-abi integration-test suite from passing. Doesn't trip any SIGABRT or memory corruption.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T19:26:39.292180364Z","created_by":"cc-libc","updated_at":"2026-04-22T19:34:02Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T19:34:02Z","close_reason":"Accepted host-initialized pthread_condattr_t via host getclock fallback; workspace now advances to glibc_internal_abi_test allocator abort","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["abi","condvar","deterministic","full-review","pthread","test"]}

 {"id":"bd-am9u.3","title":"Increase CPU affinity mask from 128 bytes (1024 CPUs) to support larger systems","description":"FILE: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs:1804
CURRENT: let mut mask = [0u8; 128] — supports max 1024 CPUs.
PROBLEM: Large servers can have >1024 logical CPUs (e.g., AMD EPYC with 256 cores + SMT = 512, multi-socket = 1024+). The mask should be sized to the actual system or use a reasonable upper bound.
FIX: Either use a larger static buffer (e.g., 512 bytes = 4096 CPUs) or dynamically query the kernel for the actual affinity mask size. The sched_getaffinity syscall returns the actual size used, so we could do a two-pass approach.
IMPACT: Low — only affects very large servers. Current 1024 CPU limit is sufficient for most systems.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:03:53.388076511Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:14:19.511676710Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:14:19.510452740Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-am9u.3","depends_on_id":"bd-am9u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:03:53.388076511Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-am9u.4","title":"Make default thread stack size configurable via environment variable","description":"FILES: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:2488,2494
CURRENT: ATTR_DEFAULT_STACK_SIZE = 2 * 1024 * 1024 (2 MiB), ATTR_MIN_STACK_SIZE = 16384 (16 KiB)
CONTEXT: glibc uses a default of 8 MiB, musl uses 128 KiB. Our 2 MiB is a reasonable middle ground but differs from both.
FIX: Keep 2 MiB as default but allow override via FRANKENLIBC_THREAD_STACK_SIZE env var. The minimum should match PTHREAD_STACK_MIN from the system.
IMPACT: Low — 2 MiB works for most applications. Some embedded or memory-constrained scenarios might want smaller stacks.
CONSIDERATION: Don't read env var on every thread creation — cache it at init time.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:03:54.503610731Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:15:51.871640506Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:15:51.870681077Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-am9u.4","depends_on_id":"bd-am9u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:03:54.503610731Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-am9u.5","title":"Fix _SC_TTY_NAME_MAX from 32 to match system reality","description":"FILE: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs:1924
CURRENT: _SC_TTY_NAME_MAX = 32
PROBLEM: While glibc returns 32 for this, some systems have longer TTY names (especially for pseudo-terminals with long paths like /dev/pts/XXX). The constant should match glibc's behavior for compatibility.
FIX: Keep 32 to match glibc behavior but add a comment documenting the rationale. This is actually correct for glibc compat.
IMPACT: Very low — TTY names are almost always < 32 chars.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:05.574339644Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:16:15.748586062Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:16:14.432724344Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-am9u.5","depends_on_id":"bd-am9u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:05.574339644Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1339,"issue_id":"bd-am9u.5","author":"ubuntu","text":"Verified: _SC_TTY_NAME_MAX=32 matches glibc behavior. No change needed.","created_at":"2026-03-25T05:16:15Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-amm13","title":"PORT: radixsort/sradixsort (NetBSD libutil radix sort family)","description":"Port the NetBSD libutil radixsort(3) family: radixsort(base,nmemb,table,endbyte) and sradixsort(base,nmemb,table,endbyte). Both sort an array of pointers to byte strings (terminated by endbyte) using an optional 256-entry translation table that remaps each input byte to a sort key. sradixsort guarantees stable order of equal keys; radixsort does not. Implementation: pure-safe core radix_sort(items: &[&[u8]], table: Option<&[u8;256]>) returning a permutation Vec<usize> using stable sort with per-position translated-byte comparison; abi shims that walk each pointer to find endbyte, build slices, sort, and rewrite the pointer array in place. Tests: identity table, reverse-order table, NULL table = byte order, embedded-bytes preserved at endbyte boundary, stability for sradixsort, length-difference ordering, NULL/zero edge cases.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T08:04:31.204056311Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T08:04:31.204056311Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-amm13","title":"PORT: radixsort/sradixsort (NetBSD libutil radix sort family)","description":"Port the NetBSD libutil radixsort(3) family: radixsort(base,nmemb,table,endbyte) and sradixsort(base,nmemb,table,endbyte). Both sort an array of pointers to byte strings (terminated by endbyte) using an optional 256-entry translation table that remaps each input byte to a sort key. sradixsort guarantees stable order of equal keys; radixsort does not. Implementation: pure-safe core radix_sort(items: &[&[u8]], table: Option<&[u8;256]>) returning a permutation Vec<usize> using stable sort with per-position translated-byte comparison; abi shims that walk each pointer to find endbyte, build slices, sort, and rewrite the pointer array in place. Tests: identity table, reverse-order table, NULL table = byte order, embedded-bytes preserved at endbyte boundary, stability for sradixsort, length-difference ordering, NULL/zero edge cases.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T08:04:31.204056311Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T08:09:57.036127094Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T08:09:57.034129710Z","close_reason":"shipped: radixsort + sradixsort with optional 256-byte translation table + endbyte sentinel + 7 core tests + 11 abi tests, all green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-anoe7","title":"fuzz: pathname encoding edge cases (NUL, UTF-8, long, .. traversal, symlinks)","description":"## Scope
Add crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_pathname.rs targeting the pathname-parsing + canonicalization surface: realpath, canonicalize_file_name, basename/dirname, access/faccessat, stat/lstat/fstatat, readlink/readlinkat. These share a common pathname-bytes input channel and are the classic CVE surface (CVE-2018-1000001 realpath OOB, CVE-2016-1234 glibc glob).

## Archetype
1 + 3: Crash-detector on the impl + **differential** against libc for path operations that don't touch fs state (basename/dirname) and for the few that do (stat/readlink) over a pre-seeded tmpdir with a known symlink graph.

## Inputs
struct PathnameFuzzInput {
  op: u8,                 // 0=realpath, 1=basename, 2=dirname, 3=stat, 4=lstat, 5=readlink, 6=access
  bytes: Vec<u8>,         // arbitrary — rejected if it contains interior NUL (CString won't build)
  buf_size: u16,          // for readlink
  flags: i32,             // for faccessat etc.
}

## Invariants
- Never panic
- For basename/dirname: result string length <= original + some constant; bytes are a suffix/prefix of the input
- For realpath: if non-null, resolved path has no '//' or '/./' or trailing '/', all components are canonical
- For stat family: non-negative rc implies st_dev/st_ino written; errno on failure is in the documented set
- Host parity: our errno family matches libc for inert paths

## Seed corpus
- empty, '/', '.', '..', '/tmp', '/proc/self', '/dev/null', '/etc/passwd', '/////' (collapsing slashes), '../../../..', path with unicode (é, 中, emoji), path with high-bit bytes, path at NAME_MAX (255), path at PATH_MAX (4096)

## Expected bug classes
- PATH_MAX overflow, NAME_MAX overflow, NUL-embedded path, UTF-8 boundary split, symlink loop detection, '..' collapsing vs preserving, trailing-slash semantics, basename('') = '', dirname('/') = '/'

## Score
5 × 4 × /3 ≈ 6.7 — high (path CVEs are a historical goldmine).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"cc-libc","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:39:57.580798569Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T06:05:18.036940066Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T06:05:18.036940066Z","close_reason":"Added seeded pathname fuzz coverage, canonical path assertions, and host return/errno parity checks for basename, dirname, stat/lstat, access, fstatat, readlink/readlinkat, and faccessat.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-anoe7","depends_on_id":"bd-jy4qu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:01:45.216777141Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-at1","title":"CONFORMANCE: libc *at variants diff matrix","description":"Differential harness shipped (see commit). 7 functions: openat (AT_FDCWD + real dirfd), mkdirat+unlinkat AT_REMOVEDIR round-trip, unlinkat without AT_REMOVEDIR on dir EISDIR, renameat (cross-impl, separate file pairs), symlinkat+readlinkat round-trip with content match, linkat (hardlink). 8 tests, 0 divergences against glibc.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T19:30:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T19:30:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T19:30:00Z","close_reason":"Harness shipped; 0 divergences.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["at-variants","conformance","differential"]}
